perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's senior management with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will not prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party. For purposes of this paragraph, "solicit for employment" shall not be deemed to include any general solicitations of employment by one Party not specifically directed towards employees of the other Party. Nothing herein shall prevent either Party from, directly or indirectly, soliciting for employment or hiring any member of the other Party's senior management whose employment with such other Party has been terminated.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of six (6) months from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's senior management with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will not prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party. For purposes of this paragraph, "solicit for employment" shall not be deemed to include any general solicitations of employment by one Party not specifically directed towards employees of the other Party. Nothing herein shall prevent either Party from, directly or indirectly, soliciting for employment or hiring any member of the other Party's senior management whose employment with such other Party has been terminated.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the non-solicitation period from 'one (1) year' to 'six (6) months' creates an in-text contradiction with section 7, which states the agreement continues for 'one (1) year from the Effective Date'. This inconsistency leads to uncertainty about which duration applies to the non-solicitation clause, potentially undermining the agreement's protection of each party's senior management.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall become effective on the date first set forth above and shall continue for the longer of (i) one (1) year from the Effective Date or (ii) one (1) year after the termination of any definitive agreement entered into by the Parties with respect to the Transaction, except as otherwise explicitly stated herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the non-solicitation period from 'one (1) year' to 'six (6) months'. The contradicted text states that the agreement continues for 'one (1) year from the Effective Date'. This creates a direct inconsistency in the duration of the agreement's terms, specifically regarding the non-solicitation clause. The change makes the two sections incompatible, creating a strong in-text contradiction and thus satisfying the condition for a 'YES' response.

perturbation.original_text: The term "affiliate" means any person or entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "affiliate" means any person or entity controlling, controlled by or under common influence with a Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'common control' to 'common influence' in the definition of 'affiliate' broadens the scope significantly. This creates an in-text contradiction with the responsibilities outlined in section 2, as 'influence' is subjective and difficult to quantify, leading to uncertainty about the extent of liability for actions of these newly defined 'affiliates'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and for the purposes of the ongoing Transaction, and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties, using the same degree of care used to protect its own proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its affiliates, its employees, its representatives and its consultants, in each case if such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants have a need to know, and providing such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants (i) use the Confidential Information for the purposes of the Transaction only, and (ii) are bound to protect the Confidential Information to the same extent as the Receiving Party is bound. The Parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants and hereby agree, at their sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. The term "affiliate" means any person or entity controlling, controlled by or under common influence with a Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'affiliate' based on 'control,' which is a relatively clear and objective standard. The changed text broadens the definition to include 'common influence,' which is subjective and less well-defined. The contradicted text holds the Receiving Party responsible for breaches by its affiliates. By expanding the definition of 'affiliate' to include those under 'common influence,' the Receiving Party's liability for the actions of these newly defined 'affiliates' becomes ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This creates a direct conflict because the original scope of responsibility was tied to a defined term that has now been expanded significantly, causing uncertainty and potential disputes regarding liability for 'affiliates' under 'common influence'. Because the change in text introduces the concept of 'influence' that triggers the responsibility from the contradicted text, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (excluding information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Excluding 'information in computer software or held in electronic storage media' from the definition of Confidential Information, it creates an in-text contradiction with section 5. It creates ambiguity as to who owns the computer software or data held in electronic storage media. The effect is that while the rest of the confidential information disclosed under the agreement is to remain property of the disclosing party, any computer software or data in electronic storage media does not, creating an in-text contradiction with the rest of the stated terms. Now Section 5 has a contradictory condition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (excluding information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party at any time, shall promptly return or destroy all such tangible Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in its possession, and the Receiving Party shall thereafter retain no such Confidential Information in any form. The Receiving Party shall be fully responsible for the return or destruction of all Confidential Information disclosed to its affiliates, its employees, it representatives and/or its consultants.
perturbation.justification: The changed text excludes 'information in computer software or held in electronic storage media' from the definition of Confidential Information. The contradicted text also excludes 'information in computer software or held in electronic storage media' from the definition of Confidential Information, but also says that the receiving party must return or destroy all such tangible Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in its possession, and the Receiving Party shall thereafter retain no such Confidential Information in any form. This creates a contradiction because, according to the changed text, the definition of 'Confidential Information' no longer encompasses computer software and data in electronic storage media, and therefore, there is no clear obligation to return or destroy those specific types of information. This fulfills the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be an original, and all of which shall constitute the same Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be an original, and all of which shall constitute independent Agreements.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'the same Agreement' to 'independent Agreements' directly contradicts the fundamental principle of a contract: that all counterparts form a single, unified agreement. Now each counter part would not share the same contract terms. This change introduces an in-text contradiction as Section 12 (a) states that is the complete Agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement: (a) is the complete Agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any and all prior Agreements, understandings or discussions with respect to the subject matter hereof; (b) shall not be construed to create any obligation on the part of either Party to complete the Transaction or to compensate the other Party in any manner, except as may be set forth by a separate written Agreement duly executed and delivered by the Parties; (c) may not be amended or in any manner modified except in a writing signed by the Parties; and (d) shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the United States and, in particular, the State of New York except its rules as to choice of law. The Parties agree and hereby consent to the jurisdiction and venue of the state and federal courts for the Borough of Manhattan, New York, New York. If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remainder shall be enforced as fully as possible and the unenforceable provisions shall be deemed modified to the limited extent required to permit its enforcement in a manner most closely representing the intention of the Parties as expressed herein. Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to the Parties, the Parties agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or the affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants of either Party and, accordingly, that the Parties shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and/or specific performance, if either Party (including the affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants thereof) breaches or threatens to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be an original, and all of which shall constitute independent Agreements.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that all counterparts constitute 'the same Agreement'. The changed text modifies this to 'independent Agreements'. This directly contradicts section (a) of the contradicted text, which explicitly states that 'This Agreement...is the complete Agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof'. The change introduces an in-text contradiction, because if the counterparts are independent agreements, then the original agreement is not the complete agreement. Therefore, the change causes a direct conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its affiliates, its employees, its representatives and its consultants, in each case if such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants have a need to know, and providing such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants (i) use the Confidential Information for the purposes of the Transaction only, and (ii) are bound to protect the Confidential Information to the same extent as the Receiving Party is bound.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its employees and its representatives, in each case if such employees and representatives have a need to know, and providing such employees and representatives (i) use the Confidential Information for the purposes of the Transaction only, and (ii) are bound to protect the Confidential Information to the same extent as the Receiving Party is bound.
perturbation.explanation: Removing affiliates and consultants from the scope of authorized disclosure creates an in-text contradiction within Section 2. By removing the ability for the receiving party to disclose confidential information to 'affiliates' and 'consultants' and changing only the first paragraph of Section 2, it creates a contradictory paragraph in the following section: 'The Parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants'. The initial paragraph says that the confidential information can not be disclosed to the receiving party's affiliates or consultants, while the proceeding text states that the disclosing party is responsible for the actions of it's affiliates and consultants.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and for the purposes of the ongoing Transaction, and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties, using the same degree of care used to protect its own proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its employees and its representatives, in each case if such employees and representatives have a need to know, and providing such employees and representatives (i) use the Confidential Information for the purposes of the Transaction only, and (ii) are bound to protect the Confidential Information to the same extent as the Receiving Party is bound. The Parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants and hereby agree, at their sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. The term "affiliate" means any person or entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Receiving Party to disclose Confidential Information to affiliates and consultants. The changed text removes 'affiliates' and 'consultants' from the scope of authorized disclosure. The contradicted text states 'The Parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants'. This creates a contradiction because the modified section limits disclosure to only employees and representatives, while the contradicted section implies responsibility for actions of affiliates and consultants, even though the modified text no longer authorizes disclosure to them. This creates a direct conflict within the document and makes the obligations unclear.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree and hereby consent to the jurisdiction and venue of the state and federal courts for the Borough of Manhattan, New York, New York.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree and hereby consent to the jurisdiction and venue of the state courts for the Borough of Manhattan, New York, New York, but federal courts will be used for resolution.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of the clause 'but federal courts will be used for resolution' creates an in-text contradiction within the same sentence and with the overall agreement regarding jurisdiction and venue. It causes an contradiction between the parties original terms, resulting in legal uncertainty and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement: (a) is the complete Agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any and all prior Agreements, understandings or discussions with respect to the subject matter hereof; (b) shall not be construed to create any obligation on the part of either Party to complete the Transaction or to compensate the other Party in any manner, except as may be set forth by a separate written Agreement duly executed and delivered by the Parties; (c) may not be amended or in any manner modified except in a writing signed by the Parties; and (d) shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the United States and, in particular, the State of New York except its rules as to choice of law. The Parties agree and hereby consent to the jurisdiction and venue of the state courts for the Borough of Manhattan, New York, New York, but federal courts will be used for resolution. If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remainder shall be enforced as fully as possible and the unenforceable provisions shall be deemed modified to the limited extent required to permit its enforcement in a manner most closely representing the intention of the Parties as expressed herein. Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to the Parties, the Parties agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or the affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants of either Party and, accordingly, that the Parties shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and/or specific performance, if either Party (including the affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants thereof) breaches or threatens to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be an original, and all of which shall constitute independent Agreements.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement regarding jurisdiction. The original text specifies agreement to both state and federal courts in Manhattan. The changed text agrees to state courts but then introduces a clause stating 'but federal courts will be used for resolution', which directly contradicts the initial consent to state court jurisdiction. This contradiction exists within the same sentence and impacts the agreement as a whole as the parties consented to state court only. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the agreed-upon forum for dispute resolution, as well as violating the terms of the agreement, making it a strong contradiction. This adheres to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' and is a strong contradiction, as the 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties', leading to a dispute over contractual obligations.
